2604.27007.title: Binary Spiking Neural Networks as Causal Models
2604.27007.paper_summary: We provide a causal analysis of Binary Spiking Neural Networks (BSNNs) to explain their behavior. We formally define a BSNN and represent its spiking activity as a binary causal model. Thanks to this causal representation, we are able to explain the output of the network by leveraging logic-based methods. In particular, we show that we can successfully use a SAT as well as a SMT solver to compute abductive explanations from this binary causal model. To illustrate our approach, we trained the BSNN on the standard MNIST dataset and applied our SAT-based and SMT-based methods to finding abductive explanations of the network's classifications based on pixel-level features. We also compared the found explanations against SHAP, a popular method used in the area of explainable AI. We show that, unlike SHAP, our approach guarantees that a found explanation does not contain completely irrelevant features.
2604.27007.paper_summary_zh: 我們提供了二元脈衝神經網路（BSNNs）的因果分析，以解釋其行為。<lb/>我們正式定義了BSNN，並將其脈衝活動表示為一個二元因果模型。<lb/>得益於這種因果表示，我們能夠利用基於邏輯的方法解釋網路的輸出。<lb/>特別是，我們展示了如何成功使用SAT以及SMT求解器從這個二元因果模型中計算出推斷解釋。<lb/>為了說明我們的方法，我們在標準的MNIST數據集上訓練了BSNN，並應用我們基於SAT和SMT的方法來尋找基於像素級特徵的網路分類的推斷解釋。<lb/>我們還將找到的解釋與SHAP進行了比較，SHAP是一種在可解釋人工智慧領域中使用的流行方法。<lb/>我們展示了，與SHAP不同，我們的方法保證找到的解釋不包含完全無關的特徵。
2604.27007.author: Aditya Kar et.al.
2604.27007.authors: Aditya Kar, Emiliano Lorini, Timothée Masquelier
